{"title":"CDM-Landing Page Links Main","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":10,"markdown":"**Hardware Asset Management (HWAM)** | Devices found on agency networks

▸ [Hardware Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/4bcb0fc0-09d0-11ed-bd84-33153167e15c#)  

---

**High Value Assets (HVA)** | Details on the agencies’ critical information systems in a consolidated HVA Dashboard

 ▸[HVA Devices](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/e889c7d0-5efb-11ee-ab05-71cf3e789c61)  
 ▸[HVA Software](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/1a1cb8b0-9f3f-11ee-ae0a-450e1d1025ea)  
 ▸[HVA Misconfigurations](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/592c4970-afda-11ee-b534-dfb0d7976a5a)  
 ▸[HVA Vulnerabilities](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/b50dd990-6d02-11ee-a7ac-eb84a370e59e)  
⭐▸[HVA System Boundaries](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/10ac5643-c368-43f1-8251-929ce5297b91) 

---

**Software Asset Management (SWAM)** | Software found on agency networks

▸[Software Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/9807e500-0919-11ed-bd84-33153167e15c)

---

**Configuration Settings Management (CSM)** | Misconfigurations found on agency networks

▸[Misconfiguration Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/c2fa8940-09d0-11ed-bd84-33153167e15c)

---

**Vulnerability Management (VUL)** | Vulnerabilities discovered on agency networks

▸[Vulnerability Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/66833a00-0916-11ed-9940-d955314b400b)

**Remediated Vulnerabilities** | Summarizes organizations remediation performance over the past 90 days for all vulnerabilities, KEVs, and those with severity of high/critical to include Mean Time to Remediate (MTTR) metrics

⭐▸[Remediated Vulnerabilities](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/4597b731-8fcb-43f6-aa9b-46159428f804)


**Priority Risks** | This dashboard will contain all panels that give centralized information about risk prioritization with metrics on open vulnerabilities, high/critical findings, KEV findings, and SSVC's Exploitation Status, and more.

⭐▸[Priority Risks](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/6b48e19c-c987-4094-901a-78ee82269654)



**SSVC Overview** | Stakeholder-Specific Vulnerability Categorization (SSVC) triage process data related to CVEs. Includes Exploitation, Automatable, and Technical Impact.

▸[SSVC Overview](/s/fed2/app/dashboards#/view/b350410f-c5fa-4468-9a35-ab153b0462c8)


---

**Container Hierarchy** | Has two types of CDM containers: the OU Container (for example, under DHS, OUs might include components such as Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Customs and Border Protection, etc.) is used for organization-specific policies and attributes, while the FISMA Container is used for security policies and attributes that are required for compliance with FISMA

▸[Federal Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/7a339ff0-09ce-11ed-9940-d955314b400b)  

---

**Federal Information Security Modernization Act (FISMA)** | Key FISMA metrics to simplify the reporting process

⭐▸[1. Enumerating the Environment](/s/fisma/app/kibana#/dashboard/59ae474c-2056-4054-b480-bcc59d83346a)   
▸[5. Implementing IPv6](/s/fisma/app/kibana#/dashboard/407b2eec-ac6f-4077-b74e-c5228b44d7c3)  
▸[8. Smart Patching](/s/fisma/app/kibana#/dashboard/be39c4a1-a91f-4348-a634-ab313ae507b4)  
▸[FISMA Reference](/s/fisma/app/kibana#/dashboard/3cda1607-36d1-442c-b34b-9e0f5ce193b0) 

---

**Emergency Directives (ED) and Binding Operational Directives (BOD)**  | Facilitates compliance with Emergency Directives (EDs) and Binding Operational Directives (BODs)

⭐▸[Directives Navigator](/s/directives/app/kibana#/dashboard/78a65c12-6f5d-4107-b522-363c6714df30) 

---

**Cyber Hygiene (CyHy)** | Establishes visibility into risk postures for publicly facing assets

⭐▸[CyHy Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/6bd93ba5-c63e-4ad5-9f83-f815f70506a0)  
⭐▸[BOD 18-01](/s/directives/app/kibana#/dashboard/f2161cc3-d697-4b25-921c-be494a6d2e56)

---

**Asset Inventory Management (AIM)** | Measure of the reliability of agency asset management data

▸[AIM  Score Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/0401d180-af3c-11ed-84a9-8fda8c5e892a) 

---

**Secure Cloud Business Applications (SCuBA)** | Highlights misconfigurations from baseline, secure standards for common cloud services

▸[SCuBA Overview](/s/fed2/app/kibana#/dashboard/d5237989-e551-4fe9-aafc-32c7ba7c4810)  
▸[BOD 25-01: Implementing Secure Practices for Cloud Services](/s/directives/app/kibana#/dashboard/d1495b9d-14fb-443e-a501-02f1202318ee)

---

**Attack Surface Management (ASM)** | Continuous discovery, analysis, prioritization, remediation and monitoring of the cybersecurity vulnerabilities and potential attack vectors that make up an organization’s attack surface

⭐▸[BOD 23-02: Implementation Guidance for Mitigating the Risk from Internet-Exposed Management Interfaces](/s/directives/app/kibana#/dashboard/91a13c3b-43a8-4c62-963f-96232ca29884)

---

attributes.description: Contains information about the remediation of vulnerabilities by agencies.  Summarizes the organization’s remediation performance over the past 90 days for all vulnerabilities, KEVs, and those with severity of high/critical. MTTR metrics provide insight into how quickly teams are addressing threats, while daily remediation trends offer visibility into operational tempo and progress. This view supports leadership oversight and helps track compliance, readiness, and overall security posture.

{"title":"M 21-07 Information","type":"markdown","params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"### M 21-07 - Completing the Transition to Internet Protocol Version 6 (IPv6)
https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/M-21-07.pdf
 
* At least 20% of IP-enabled assets on Federal networks are operating in IPv6-only environments by the end of FY 2023
* At least 50% of IP-enabled assets on Federal networks are operating in IPv6-only environments by the end of FY 2024
* At least 80% of IP-enabled assets on Federal networks are operating in IPv6-only environments by the end of FY 2025"},"aggs":[]}

attributes.title: HVA Misconfigurations

attributes.description: List of top twenty open vulnerabilities by CVE ID with the highest count (severity level of "High" or "Critical") found on a device designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). HVA Device count is independent of whether the device belongs to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA High or Critical Open Vulnerabilities with Highest Count

attributes.description: List of Agencies by ID, total count of open CVEs designated as "Known Exploited Vulnerabilities" (KEVs), unique count of open CVEs designated as KEVs, total count of open vulnerabilities with the severity level of "High" or "Critical", total count of open vulnerabilities, and unique count of open vulnerabilities. All counts are found on devices designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). HVA Device count is independent of whether the device belongs to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA Open Vulnerability Counts

attributes.description: Total count of open vulnerabilities (aggregated by severity level) found on a device designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). "UNKNOWN" and "-" values for severity are considered unknown, as there is either a null value or a value that does not match the severity enumerated list. These are assets that belong to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA Open Vulnerability Severity Levels

attributes.description: List of Agencies by ID and total count of open vulnerabilities by severity level found on a device designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). "UNKNOWN" and "-" values for severity are considered unknown, as there is either a null value or a value that does not match the severity enumerated list. HVA Device count is independent of whether the device belongs to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA Open Vulnerability Severity Level Counts

attributes.description: List of Agencies by ID and total count of remediated vulnerabilities (aggregated by time periods) found on a device designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). HVA Device count is independent of whether the device belongs to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA Remediated Vulnerabilities

attributes.description: List of Agencies by ID and total count of vulnerabilities by severity level found on a device designated as a High Value Asset (HVA). "UNKNOWN" and "-" values for severity are considered unknown, as there is either a null value or a value that does not match the severity enumerated list. These are assets that belong to a System Boundary designated as HVA.
attributes.title: HVA Unique Open Vulnerability Severity Level Counts

attributes.description: Information about vulnerabilities that belong to System Boundaries that are designated as High Value Assets (HVAs). Helps assess vulnerability (VUL) information of vulnerabilities associated with HVA systems.

attributes.description: Summary of results for various applications as to whether their policies failed, had warnings, passed, need manual checks, tests were omitted, if the tests had errors, or if the tests had an incorrect result, and shows the total number of tests.

params.formula: count(control.result, kql='control.result : "Incorrect result" OR control.result: "Error" OR control.result: "Fail"  OR control.result : "N/A" OR control.result : "Omitted" OR control.result : "Pass" OR control.result : "Warning" ')

filter.query: control.result : "Incorrect result" OR control.result: "Error" OR control.result: "Fail"  OR control.result : "N/A" OR control.result : "Omitted" OR control.result : "Pass" OR control.result : "Warning"

attributes.title: SCuBA Security Baseline Conformance Reports - All

attributes.description: Latest tenant information, including tenant name, agency, tool version, and date of the report.
attributes.title: Tenant Information - All

attributes.description: Detailed baseline results for various applications. Includes result of the policy check, control ID, requirement description of the control, criticality of the control, details about the policy check, the policy URL, and scan date.
attributes.title: SCuBA Security Baseline Reports - All

attributes.description: Incorrect results for various applications. Includes comments, resolution date, original result, details about original results, the tenant, Tier 0 organization ID, Tier 1 Organization ID, and scan date.
attributes.title: SCuBA Security Baseline Reports - Incorrect Results - All

attributes.description: Count of licenses in use and total licenses by the license SKU ID pertaining to Entra ID.

attributes.title: cdm_scuba_m365_conditional_access_policy_history

attributes.description: Conditional access policies information pertaining to the Entra ID application. Policy name, current state, users, apps/actions, conditions, block/grant access, session controls, and scan date.
attributes.title: Entra ID - Conditional Access Policies - All

Tenant Inventory Data

attributes.name: SCuBA Tenant Inventory
attributes.title: cdm_scuba_cyberscope_tenant

attributes.description:
Tenant Inventory breakdown by Organizational Unit and Tenant ID, limited to tenants in scope (excluding in scope unknown).

The table displays runtime fields: Inventory Age, SCuBA Results Age, Results This Fiscal Qtr, and Fully Compliant.

Fully Compliant is true if the record has results, is in-scope for BOD, the latest scan is in the current fiscal quarter, and the inventory scan age <= 365 days.

meta.alias: In Scope and not Scope Unknown

attributes.title: SCuBA Overview Tenant Inventory

attributes.description: DHS CISA provides the service Cyber Hygiene (CyHy) Vulnerability Scanning (VS) to participating agencies for regular network and vulnerability scans to proactively identify vulnerabilities directly accessible for exploitation by an external party from the Internet. This service allows CISA to detect and report on vulnerabilities from a perspective similar to that of an attacker.

attributes.title: SSVC - Detail Information
attributes.visState:
{"title":"SSVC - Detail Information","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":9,"openLinksInNewTab":true,"markdown":"| Triage Point | Description | Values |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Exploitation** | State of the exploitation, evidence of active exploitation of a vulnerability. | NONE, POC, or ACTIVE |
| **Automatable** | The ease and speed with which a cyber threat actor can cause exploitation events. | YES or NO |
| **Technical Impact** | Technical impact of exploiting the vulnerability. | PARTIAL or TOTAL |

_To find more information related to SSVC data, explore the [SSVC Calculator ↗](https://www.cisa.gov/ssvc-calculator)._"}}

attributes.description: AIM Score calculated using summation of "Average CDM device counts from the last 30 calendar days" from all displayed agencies divided by summation of "Average of device counts of most recent 3 FISMA reporting cycles" from all displayed agencies.

tinymathAst.name: ifelse
tinymathAst.text:
ifelse(
    sum(fisma_9M_avg) == 0, 
    1, 
    sum(cdm_30d_avg) / pick_max( sum(fisma_9M_avg), 1)
)

attributes.title: AIM Score

attributes.description: Average of daily CDM device counts from the last 30 calendar days.
attributes.title: CDM 30 Day Average Device Count

attributes.description: Average count of FISMA Self-Reported Devices (for FISMA metrics 1.2 from the most recent 3 FISMA reports).
attributes.title: FISMA Self-Reported Devices

attributes.description: List of agencies, CFO Status, average of daily CDM device counts from the last 30 calendar days, average count of FISMA Self-Reported Devices (for FISMA metrics 1.2 from the most recent 3 FISMA reports), and AIM score (count of devices in CDM divided by count of FISMA Self-Reported Devices).
attributes.title: Agency AIM Scores
Calculating the AIM Score
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Calculating the AIM Score","type":"metrics","aggs":[],"params":{"time_range_mode":"last_value","id":"61ca57f0-469d-11e7-af02-69e470af7417","type":"markdown","series":[{"time_range_mode":"entire_time_range","id":"61ca57f1-469d-11e7-af02-69e470af7417","color":"#68BC00","split_mode":"everything","split_color_mode":"kibana","metrics":[{"id":"61ca57f2-469d-11e7-af02-69e470af7417","type":"count"}],"separate_axis":0,"axis_position":"right","formatter":"number","chart_type":"line","line_width":1,"point_size":1,"fill":0.5,"stacked":"none"}],"time_field":"last_updated","index_pattern":"data_dictionary","interval":"","axis_position":"left","axis_formatter":"number","axis_scale":"normal","show_legend":1,"show_grid":1,"tooltip_mode":"show_all","isModelInvalid":false,"markdown":"#### AIM score for an agency is calculated using the following numerator and denominator:
**Numerator**: average of daily CDM device counts from the last 30 calendar days.  It should not include days agencies don’t report anything in the calculation of the average (i.e. don’t want agencies to be penalized with 0 day count).  

**Denominator**: average of the agency's FISMA metrics 1.2 from the most recent 3 FISMA reports. It should only look at the numbers from the last 3 Quarters.  The average should not include 0 count/quarter if agencies miss a reporting cycle.

1. If CDM data exists but no FISMA metrics from Cyberscope, then the AIM score is 100%
2. If CDM data does not exist, then the AIM score is 0%","background_color":null,"markdown_css":"","bar_color_rules":[{"id":"54220760-785d-11eb-a54c-71b7f0b96dc2"}],"use_kibana_indexes":false,"hide_last_value_indicator":true,"drop_last_bucket":0,"truncate_legend":1,"max_lines_legend":1}}

attributes.description: This dashboard will contain information about the AIM score for agencies. The AIM score is used to assess the reliability of asset inventories by comparing Cyberscope FISMA metrics to CDM device counts.
